mnemonics.title: Ocean Zones Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Remember ocean zones with: 'Every Man's Bad Alphabet Has' - Epipelagic, Mesopelagic, Bathypelagic, Abyssopelagic, Hadopelagic (from shallow to deep)

mnemonics.title: Marine Food Chain Order
mnemonics.content: Producers → Primary Consumers → Secondary Consumers → Tertiary Consumers → Apex Predators. Think: 'Please Cook Tasty Apples' for Phytoplankton → Crustaceans → Tiny Fish → Apex predators

acronyms.expansion: Convention on the Law of the Sea
acronyms.usage: International treaty governing ocean resources and boundaries
Maritime zone where a country has sovereign rights over resources
UNESCO body coordinating ocean research and education

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a vertical cross-section: top layer (light blue, sunny) → darker blue layer → pitch black at bottom. Draw a triangle representing depth increasing to the right.

visualTips.visualize: Picture a pyramid: wide base = phytoplankton, middle = zooplankton and small fish, top = large predators. Arrows point upward showing energy flow.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a circular motion: deep cold water rises on one side (like water in a pot heated from below), bringing nutrients up to the surface where phytoplankton bloom.

analogies.analogy: Just as lungs exchange gases in our body, oceans absorb CO₂ and release oxygen, keeping Earth's atmosphere in balance.
Just as grasslands support land ecosystems, phytoplankton are the 'green meadows' of the ocean that feed the entire marine world.
Like cities teeming with diverse populations, coral reefs host millions of organisms in complex structures, creating underwater 'neighborhoods' for marine life.
Think of ocean currents like giant conveyor belts circulating heat around Earth, distributing warm and cold water just as暖气 distributes heat in a building.
